coverage: 100% of reachable node types across the 20-group, 319-node root closure; three evaluated dowel dependencies are included.
validation: Authored output and five non-cutter control variants have exact topology and bounds. The default's 97,008 serialized display triangles now also match Blender index-for-index and winding-for-winding after correcting Curve-to-Mesh endpoint cap orientation. The reconstructed Generated-coordinate noise/Wave bump and Window-coordinate diagonal Backfacing emission reach 0.9951 silhouette IoU, complete mutual one-pixel coverage, 0.00425 RGB MAE, and 0.9979 macro luminance correlation. PRINT VIZ 1OCT2024 stores the authored 0.22 mm layer-height value and includes an optional cross-section inspection branch, but that branch is disabled in the captured default and the supplied project contains no slicer toolpath or source G-code. Cutter mode uses a browser-native open-surface Boolean because Blender's FLOAT solver subtracts three overlapping closed pipes from three open shells, an operation a solid-only Manifold cannot represent. It retains exact bounds and every sampled Blender point and triangle centroid lies on the browser surface at zero measured distance. The browser keeps a small extra inner patch (3.1% surface-area overhead): 27,136 vertices / 53,872 triangles versus Blender's 29,705 vertices / 27,431 polygons (52,605 triangles at the probed Boolean stage).
